text:
Team GB teenager Toby ‘The Terminator’ Roberts has clinched a historic medal at theParisOlympics, with the nation’s first in sport climbing.

The 19-year-old from Surrey secured gold in the men’s combined event after a dramatic slip-up fromJapan’s Sorato Anraku, who was forced to settle for silver.

Roberts was left in utter disbelief and had his head in his hands as he wrote his name into British Olympic history at the 2024 Paris Games.

‘I am just lost for words,’ a gobsmacked Roberts told theBBC. ‘To find out that I had got the gold in that moment was truly incredible.

‘[Anraku] is just an incredibly strong climber and it is insane how strong he is. He looked a little nervous but he is incredibly strong.

‘I have been training for this moment my whole life. To say it hasn’t sunk in is an understatement.

‘I imagine later it will be a flood of emotions. It is a goal I have been training towards this for like 10 years.’

Asked how ‘Toby Roberts, Olympic gold medallist’ sound to him, the teen added: ‘It sounds incredible!’

Anraku looked firmly set for gold having entered the event as the favourite and he didn’t seem tired or fatigued as he started climbing the wall like Spiderman.

Many felt Anraku, dubbed ‘The Octopus’, would be unbeatable but he slipped on his ascent up the 15m wall in dramatic scenes.

The Japanese star took silver, while the bronze medal went to Austrian rock climber Jakob Schubert.

Finishing ahead of both of them was Britain’s latest Olympics star, Roberts, who trained on a climbing wall in the garden of his family home ahead of the Games.

Britain’s Hamish McArthur, meanwhile, also took part in the men’s combined event and finished fifth.

title: Team GB teen dubbed 'The Terminator' clinches historic Olympic gold | Metro News
description: Team GB teen Toby 'The Terminator' Roberts has clinched a historic medal at the Paris Olympics, with the nation's first in sport climbing.
